config.title: Turkish Market Berlin - FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers for visiting the Maybachufer Turkish Market in Neukölln.

items.q: What days is the Turkish Market in Berlin open?
items.a: The Maybachufer Turkish Market (Türkischer Markt) runs on Tuesday and Friday from 11:00 to 18:30. It is closed Monday, Wednesday, Thursday and Sunday. On Saturday the same riverside hosts a separate fabric market (Stoffmarkt), 11:00 to 17:00.

items.q: Where exactly is the Maybachufer Turkish Market?
items.a: It stretches along the Maybachufer on the Neukölln bank of the Landwehrkanal, roughly between the Kottbusser bridge and Schinkestraße. The nearest U-Bahn is Schönleinstraße (U8), about a 5-minute walk; Kottbusser Tor (U8) and Hermannplatz (U7/U8) are also close.

items.q: Is the Turkish Market in Kreuzberg or Neukölln?
items.a: It is in Neukölln. The stalls sit on the Neukölln side of the canal, and the green residential side across the water is Kreuzberg. It is often called "Kreuzberg’s Turkish market" out of habit, but the market itself is a Neukölln one.

items.q: Do I need cash at the Turkish Market?
items.a: Yes. Most produce, cheese, olive and street-food stalls are cash-only. Bring smaller notes and coins; there is no reliable card option among the traders, and hunting for an ATM mid-market wastes time.

items.q: What is the best time to visit the Maybachufer market?
items.a: Late morning, around 11:30 to 13:00, is calm and best for fresh produce and unhurried browsing. Friday afternoon is the busy, lively slot with the fullest street-food scene. The final half hour before 18:30 is when leftover produce gets discounted to clear the tables.

items.q: What can you buy at the Turkish Market on Maybachufer?
items.a: Fruit and vegetables, olives, nuts, dried fruit, spices, Turkish cheese, fresh bread and flatbread, flowers, and street food such as gözleme, börek and fresh juice. There are also textile and household stalls, and the Saturday market is dedicated to fabrics.
